findings.evidence: Roadmap README.md Layer 5: '⚠ A naïve IsUnipotent that tests nilpotence in the reduced ring A is vacuous (only g=1 qualifies)'; IsGeometricallyReduced.field_iff confirms A is reduced.
findings.fix: Remove [AffineAlgGroup k A] from the section. Define unipotence in terms of nilpotence in the comodule action (needs Layer 1) or mark as a stub with TODO.
findings.issue: IsUnipotent is defined under [AffineAlgGroup k A] which requires geometrically reduced A. In a reduced ring only 0 is nilpotent, so (g-1) nilpotent forces g=1 — the predicate is vacuous, exactly the pitfall warned against in the roadmap Layer 5.

findings.evidence: Roadmap README.md Layer 0: '⚠ Pitfall: GroupLike k A is **not** the points functor'; Coalgebra/GroupLike.lean defines it as group-like elements (Δ(g)=g⊗g, ε(g)=1) forming a CommGroup.
findings.fix: Change argument type to reflect actual points (AlgPoints, once generalized to R-points).
findings.issue: IsUnipotent takes (g : GroupLike k A) but the docstring describes 'g ∈ G(k)'. GroupLike elements are characters (always commutative), not the k-points functor. The roadmap Layer 0 explicitly warns GroupLike is 'not the points functor'.

findings.evidence: Roadmap README.md Layer 0: 'Generalize AlgPoints R A := A →ₐ[k] R for every commutative k-algebra R'
findings.fix: Generalize to AlgPoints (k A R : Type*) [CommSemiring k] [Semiring A] [Algebra k A] [CommSemiring R] [Algebra k R] := A →ₐ[k] R
findings.issue: AlgPoints is restricted to k-points (A →ₐ[k] k). The roadmap Layer 0 explicitly asks for the general functor of points over any commutative k-algebra R.

findings.evidence: Line 59: unipotent_radical_trivial : True; line 65: radical_trivial : True
findings.fix: Add TODO comments acknowledging these are placeholders for the actual unipotent-radical/radical definitions from Layers 5–6.
findings.issue: ReductiveGroup.unipotent_radical_trivial and SemisimpleGroup.radical_trivial are True — every AffineAlgGroup trivially satisfies them. The class imposes no mathematical condition and has no characteristic API.

summary: IsUnipotent is mathematically vacuous under [AffineAlgGroup k A] (geometric reducedness makes the ring reduced, so only g=1 is nilpotent — exactly the pitfall the roadmap warns against). Its argument type GroupLike k A is not the points functor. AlgPoints should be general R-points per the roadmap. ReductiveGroup/SemisimpleGroup fields are True stubs with no mathematical content.
